No Poverty.reason: The course discusses property insurance as a core social safety net and addresses climate-related disaster losses, directly supporting Target 1.5 by building resilience for vulnerable populations against economic shocks.
evidence: 氣候變遷所帶來之災損頻率與強度上升
財產保險為台灣社會安全網之核心支柱
build the resilience of the poor and those in vulnerable situations

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to hunger, food security, sustainable agriculture, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to health outcomes, disease prevention, or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: While the course is education, it does not address the SDG's targets related to access to quality education, learning outcomes, or educational infrastructure for broader populations.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to gender equality, women's empowerment, or discrimination.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to water quality, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course explicitly covers insurance for renewable energy (wind, solar) and energy storage, which is crucial for financing and expanding clean energy infrastructure, aligning with Targets 7.2 and 7.a.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course strengthens the capacity and competitiveness of the insurance industry, a key financial institution, thereby supporting economic growth and access to financial services for all (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course focuses on insuring sustainable infrastructure (renewable energy, Target 9.1) and discusses technological innovations like AI, blockchain, and digital insurance within the industry (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course discusses insurance regulation and international standards (IFRS 17, ICS 2.0), which relates to improving the regulation and monitoring of global financial markets and institutions (Target 10.5).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course addresses managing economic losses from disasters through property insurance, directly supporting Target 11.5 on reducing disaster impacts and building community resilience (Target 11.b).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course explicitly discusses integrating 'ESG指標如何融入承保與投資決策流程,' directly aligning with Target 12.6 on encouraging companies to adopt sustainable practices.

Climate Action.reason: Climate change is a core theme, focusing on managing climate risks through insurance pricing, disaster loss analysis, and supporting the net-zero transition, directly addressing Targets 13.1 and 13.2.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to marine ecosystems, ocean pollution, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or topics relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, deforestation, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course focuses on insurance supervision and regulation ('監理政策'), which contributes to building effective, accountable, and transparent financial institutions as per Target 16.6.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course promotes academia-industry partnerships by inviting experts and analyzes international policies (UNEP FI, TCFD, IAIS), enhancing policy coherence for sustainable development (Targets 17.16, 17.14).
